faq.title: Star Savior
faq.subtitle: The fastest answers new and returning players ask about Star Savior

questions.question: What is Star Savior?
questions.answer: Star Savior is a character-training turn-based RPG built around squad progression, the Journey system, elemental matchups, gear growth, and long-term roster planning.

questions.question: Where can I download Star Savior?
questions.answer: Star Savior is currently highlighted on this site through the official website and the Google Play listing, with official channels also linking players to news, videos, and community updates.

questions.question: How do I redeem codes in Star Savior?
questions.answer: In Star Savior, open Account Management, tap Enter Coupon, submit a working code, tap Check, and then collect the rewards from your Mailbox.

questions.question: Should I reroll in Star Savior?
questions.answer: Star Savior rerolling is optional, but it is worthwhile if you want a stronger launch roster. Most reroll plans focus on premium launch units or universal Arcana that stay useful across Journey and general progression.

questions.question: What should beginners do first in Star Savior?
questions.answer: Most Star Savior beginner routes start with pushing Mainstream Operation, claiming mission rewards constantly, building a compact core roster, and using Starlink to avoid over-investing in too many units.

questions.question: What makes the Star Savior Journey system important?
questions.answer: Star Savior uses Journey training as one of its defining progression systems, so understanding training loops, resonance, factors, and account-wide carryover is critical for efficient long-term growth.

questions.question: What homepage topics does Star Savior Wiki focus on?
questions.answer: Star Savior Wiki focuses on codes, tier lists, reroll planning, beginner routes, Arcana, best teams, builds, gear, Journey training, boss preparation, PvP, and resource management.

questions.question: Where should I follow official Star Savior updates?
questions.answer: Use the Star Savior official website, official news page, official X account, official YouTube channel, official Discord, and the Google Play listing for the main live-service update trail.

cta.title: Ready to Start Star Savior?
cta.description: Jump into the official Star Savior community for announcements and support, or open Google Play to start building your first roster.
cta.joinGame: Play Star Savior on Google Play

modules.title: Star Savior
modules.summary: Cross-checked public Arcana rankings consistently put Elastic Acceleration, Solid Destruction, Sharp Assault, and Construct Bomb in the top band, while role priorities split cleanly between DPS, tank, and healer setups.

tierGroups.title: Top Band Core SSR Arcana
items: Elastic Acceleration
Solid Destruction
Sharp Assault
Construct Bomb

tierGroups.title: Strong Follow-Up SSR Arcana
items: Shadow Erosion
Resolute Determination
Life Stigma
Double Tap
Leisurely Counter
Insight Stigma

roleTabs.label: DPS
notes: Start from Power-first SSR pieces to keep burst and clear speed high.
Sharp Assault and Elastic Acceleration fit damage-first carry plans well.
Use follow-up Arcana to reinforce damage windows after the top tier core is locked in.

roleTabs.label: Tank
notes: Tank Arcana still want enough Power to hold threat and damage tank value.
Health and Endurance become the stability layer once the front line is set.
Solid Destruction and Construct Bomb stay valuable when the team needs durable tempo.

roleTabs.label: Healer
notes: Healers care most about staying alive through long fights and boss loops.
Life Stigma and other sustain-friendly follow-ups support multi-turn consistency.
Use SSR Arcana as the long-term healing backbone instead of overcommitting to SR fillers.

spotlight.title: Long-Term Arcana Rule
items: SSR Arcana should be the long-term core for your account.
SR Arcana mainly fill temporary stat slots early on.
Role-based sorting is the fastest way to stop wasting upgrade materials.

modules.title: Star Savior
modules.summary: Current public team guides center on 4-Savior formations, front-line tanks or sustain units, and back-line carries or debuffers, with mono-element and favorable-element matchups driving most PvE progress.

teamCards.title: Solar Party
notes: Uses favorable element pressure into Star enemies.
Keeps a front-line damage tank plus support balance.

teamCards.title: Moon Party
notes: Pairs a sturdy front with premium sustain for longer fights.
Works best when the back line can outlast Sun-targeted pressure.

teamCards.title: Star Party
notes: Leans on ranged pressure behind a stable front anchor.
Best when you want straightforward PvE progression coverage.

teamCards.title: Chaos Party
notes: Blends carry pressure with utility and sustain support.
Strong template for favorable-element swapping once your roster opens up.

modules.title: Star Savior
modules.summary: Star Savior uses Equipment rather than Relics, and the real progression curve starts when you stop over-investing in T1 filler, pivot into T2 pieces, and prioritize Speed main stats on the right slots.

priorityRows.reason: Shoes are one of the fastest ways to improve turn order for carries and supports.
Speed Necklace keeps rotations stable before you chase luxury substats.

priorityRows.mainStat: ATK% for DPS / HP% for tank-support
priorityRows.reason: Ring main stats should match the role that actually drives your clear speed or survival.
Do not over-invest in filler weapon pieces before your T2 core is ready.
Armor upgrades pay off better once your long-term T2 direction is online.
Gloves are lower priority than Speed slots during the early progression curve.

spotlight.title: T1 to T2 Progression Rule
items: The game uses a 2-tier gear structure: T1 for early progression, T2 for long-term investment.
T2 becomes the real target after Mainstream Stage 14, with some guides placing the craft unlock at Stage 15.
Finish your T2 core four before spending hard on luxury upgrades.

modules.title: Star Savior
modules.summary: Use the first week to unlock systems, raise idle income, and stabilize one real team core instead of scattering upgrades across the whole roster.

items: Finish the tutorial and claim the main launch rewards first.
Reroll only if you plan to commit to it immediately.
Choose your starter team early so your first upgrades stay focused.

timeline.focus: Push story and raise idle output
items: Push story as far as your account power allows.
Use those clears to unlock more systems and stronger idle rewards.
Start refining equipment and key skills on your best units only.

timeline.focus: Fix weak links and keep the roster compact
items: Check whether your team already has a tank, a healer or support, and two damage dealers.
Patch the weakest role in your main team before raising bench projects.
Keep daily and weekly mission routines active while farming around real bottlenecks.

timeline.focus: Prepare for the next content loop
items: Alternate farming sessions with fresh story pushes instead of forcing one mode all week.
Tune gear and Arcana around the team that actually clears content for you.
Plan how you want to spend future banner currency before the next rate-up arrives.

sidePanels.title: Core Roster Rule
items: Most week-one accounts should build around 4 to 5 units, not the full roster.
The cleanest starter shell is 1 tank, 1 healer or support, and 2 damage dealers.
Once that shell is stable, extra upgrades become much easier to justify.

sidePanels.title: Week-One Resource Discipline
items: Prioritize story unlocks because they improve both access and idle income.
Do not split gear and skill materials across too many experimental units.
Banner currency is more valuable when saved with a target pull plan in mind.

modules.title: Star Savior
modules.summary: Star Savior splits characters and Arcana into separate banners, uses strong base SSR rates, and relies on mileage exchange instead of a featured-unit guarantee system.

compareCards.title: Character Banner
compareCards.headline: Use character banners when your roster still needs a carry, tank, or premium support to unlock cleaner progression.
items: Base SSR rate is 4%.
Featured character rate is 2%.
Light and Dark featured units are listed at 1%.

compareCards.title: Arcana Banner
compareCards.headline: Arcana banners matter more once your core roster is playable and you want to sharpen stat lines, burst windows, or long-term scaling.
items: Base SSR Arcana rate is also 4%.
Featured Arcana rate is 2%.
Mileage can be saved for a future Arcana target instead of rushed.

factRows.label: Base SSR Rate
factRows.note: Both major banner types start from the same SSR base rate.

factRows.label: Featured Rate
factRows.note: Premium banners still need planning because rare featured units are not guaranteed through a split 50/50 rule.

factRows.label: Featured Guarantee
factRows.value: No 50/50 and no direct pity guarantee
factRows.note: Pity progress does not automatically hand you the featured unit on rate-up.

factRows.label: Mileage Gain
factRows.note: Every summon advances the same long-term exchange path.

factRows.label: Mileage Exchange
factRows.value: 200 mileage for the featured unit or Arcana
factRows.note: Mileage does not expire, so saving for a future banner is valid.

sidePanels.title: Starter Pull Planning
items: Pull around your most important missing role, not around every new banner.
A stronger first carry or support usually matters more than scattered summons.
If your roster is playable, saving can be better than forcing mediocre rate-up value.

sidePanels.title: Mileage Discipline
items: Treat mileage as the real long-term safety valve in this system.
Do not burn stored mileage just because it is available.
The best exchange target is usually the featured piece that closes an actual roster gap.

sidePanels.title: Filter Coverage
items: The main public filters are rarity, role, and element.
That is enough to support reroll checks, team replacement searches, and build prep without guessing.
The visible roster is anchored in currently released KR and JP entries.

sidePanels.title: Why This Database Matters
items: It is the fastest way to compare candidates before you invest resources.
It helps connect unit pages to team-building and guide decisions.
It reduces homepage friction when users only want a quick roster lookup.

modules.title: Star Savior
modules.summary: Journey is the dedicated raising mode in Star Savior, and each run feeds long-term power through training choices, condition control, and Stella Archive output.

steps.title: Set the Run Goal
steps.description: Go into Journey with a clear stat target instead of treating the mode like random side content.
items: Choose whether the run is for a damage dealer, tank, or support shell.
Plan around the stats that matter for that Savior before you spend turns.
Matching Arcana stacks are the cleanest way to keep the run pointed in one direction.

steps.title: Cycle Training and Requests
steps.description: A strong Journey run is built turn by turn through training choices, request events, and the right pacing between them.
items: Use training to push the main stat line for the role you are building.
Take requests that reinforce your intended stat or condition curve.
Avoid detours that pad weak stats without helping the final archive.

steps.title: Control Condition and Tempo
steps.description: Rest decisions are part of the route, because the run loses value quickly once condition falls behind.
items: Keep Condition at BEST whenever possible during serious runs.
Use rest as a planned reset, not as a panic button after wasted turns.
For Hard runs, STR, HP, and Endurance usually carry more value than scattered side stats.

steps.title: Lock the Stella Archive
steps.description: Every completed run becomes a Stella Archive, which works like a long-term build record for that Savior.
items: The archive preserves the value of the run for future builds.
Better Journey discipline means better archive quality over time.
That is why Journey is a core progression system, not a side mini-game.

sidePanels.title: Hard Entry Point
items: Resonance Stage 8 is the usual minimum entry target for Journey Hard.
Resonance Stage 9 is the more comfortable point if you want steadier runs.
Going in too early usually turns the mode into a resource sink instead of a growth tool.

sidePanels.title: Run Checklist
items: Three or more matching Arcana can support focused stat stacking.
Damage dealers can aim toward STR 1250, while tanks and supports can aim toward HP 1250.
For Hard mode, keep the run centered on STR, HP, Endurance, and stable condition management.

modules.title: Star Savior
modules.summary: Early raid and world-boss clears follow a repeatable loop: survive the heavy hit, strip defense, break the gauge, then detonate Nova Burst during the break window.

mechanicCards.title: Recommended Team Shell
items: Use 1 tank, 1 healer or support, 1 AoE DPS, and 1 single-target DPS for early boss consistency.
This shell keeps the run stable while still creating enough pressure for break windows.
Commonly recommended early picks include Ede for tanking and Serpent, Frey, or Elisa for sustain.

mechanicCards.title: Break Window Execution
items: Bosses enter a broken state for about two turns after the break gauge reaches zero.
Save ultimates and Nova Burst for that state rather than spending them on shielded phases.
Turn order still matters because debuffs and setup skills must land before your burst cycle starts.

mechanicCards.title: Common Failure Points
items: Most wipes come from spending ultimates before break and triggering Nova Burst too early.
Skipping a healer often causes avoidable collapses in early raid attempts.
Missing boss action-bar timing turns clean setups into low-value damage turns.

tagPanels.title: Recommended Early Picks
tags: Sustain: Serpent or Frey or Elisa
AoE: Luna or Carmen or Asela or Dana
Burst: Smile or Kira or Charles or Lacy or Tanya or Epindel

tagPanels.title: Fight-Type Adjustments
tags: Heavy add waves: strengthen the AoE slot first
DoT-heavy fights: prioritize Elisa or Frey
Shield or reflect phases: bring Smile or another defense-break unit
Track boss action bars before committing burst

modules.ctaLabel: Open boss mechanics, role picks, and break-window burst tips

modules.title: Star Savior
modules.summary: Hard Mode is a progression checkpoint for Journey and Mainstream content, with sharper stat checks, role-specific charms, and a higher practical stat ceiling.
prepChecklist: Reach Resonance Stage 8 before entering Hard Mode, with Stage 9 as the cleaner comfort point.
Clear Mainstream Stage 14 first to unlock the practical path toward T2 gear progression.
Build your carry setup around focused Arcana choices before extending side projects.
Keep your first Hard lineups compact and fully upgraded instead of rotating unfinished units.

teamCards.title: Boss-Kill Template
teamCards.composition: Ede + Frey + Smile + Kira or Charles
items: Built for stable front-line control and focused burst turns.
Use this shell when survivability is the main blocker.
Works best when your carry slot is already tuned for break-window damage.

teamCards.title: Speed-Clear Template
teamCards.composition: Sayla + Serpent + Smile + Luna or Asela
items: Prioritizes tempo and action-gauge pressure for fast stage cycles.
Best used when your roster can already survive baseline incoming damage.
Pairs well with speed-focused gear and clean turn-order setup.

charmCards.title: DPS Charm Direction
items: ATK-scaling charms are the common high-impact choice.
Pick charms that reinforce your main damage profile.
Avoid splitting charm upgrades across too many secondary carries.

charmCards.title: Tank Charm Direction
items: HP-scaling charms are usually preferred for tanks.
Stability charms are more valuable than greedy damage lines here.
Set the tank first so your burst units can keep their cycle.

charmCards.title: Support Charm Direction
items: HP-scaling charms are also preferred for supports.
Consistency beats variance in longer Hard encounters.
Prioritize uptime and survival before luxury damage options.

modules.ctaLabel: View the pre-Hard checklist, stat targets, and team templates

modules.title: Star Savior
modules.summary: Star Savior PvP is tempo-driven: speed, action-gauge control, break timing, and Nova Burst windows decide most matches.

lineupColumns.title: Offense Lineups
lineupColumns.description: Open on speed pressure and gauge control to secure the first meaningful burst turn.

entries.name: Sun Blitz
notes: Fast opener built to pressure fragile defense shells.
Best when you can force the first break window cleanly.

entries.name: Order Core
notes: Balanced control shell that can pivot between pressure and stabilizing turns.
Useful against mixed defense teams with uneven speed lines.

entries.name: Waltz Carry
entries.core: Asherah: Waltz of Starlight + Smile + Kira + Ede
notes: Center the match around one protected carry damage cycle.
Works best when your utility setup can secure burst timing.

lineupColumns.title: Defense Lineups
lineupColumns.description: Use resilient front-line structure and punish windows to deny easy offense snowball turns.

entries.name: Luna Haydee Wall
notes: Durability-first shell for slowing down high-tempo attackers.
Wins by surviving the first burst and forcing longer exchanges.

entries.name: Haydee BC Roberta
notes: Disruptive setup that punishes poor target focus from offense teams.
Most effective when your defensive turn order is tuned.

entries.name: Bell Seira Luna
notes: Hybrid defense that mixes burst threat with layered utility.
Commonly used to challenge single-plan offense rotations.

sidePanels.title: Core PvP Rules
items: Current mode coverage centers on real-time RTA and classic ladder defense matches.
A stable shell is still 2 DPS, 1 tank or frontliner, and 1 support or flex slot.
Sayla, Smile, Lou, Ede, Vesta, and Serpent are frequently highlighted as tempo anchors.

sidePanels.title: Season Rewards
items: Season-based premium currency
Upgrade materials that support progression loops
Cosmetic rewards such as titles or profile frames

modules.ctaLabel: Compare offense and defense lineups for the current PvP meta

modules.title: Star Savior
modules.summary: Progression is won by concentrating resources on one main squad, pushing your highest clear stage, and spending premium currency where long-term account value is highest.

priorityRows.rule: Push main story first to raise AFK output, then farm high-value event currency stages.
AFK rewards scale from highest cleared stage, while Journey and Training use separate lanes that should be planned together.
Resonance mats, limit-break mats, potential points, EXP, gold, Star Essence, and Operation EXP should stay aligned with your real bottleneck.
Use your best resources on the main 4 to 6 Saviors instead of spreading upgrades across side projects.
Spend Starlight Ore and summon tickets on high-impact banners rather than stamina refills or random refreshes.
Prioritize summon tickets, rare upgrade stones, and high-end gear before generic materials.

doList: Keep your upgrade path centered on one primary squad.
Spend stamina around clear-stage growth and active bottlenecks.
Plan premium pulls around banners that close concrete roster gaps.
Use event shops for high-value account progression items first.
dontList: Do not spread premium upgrades across unfinished side units.
Do not spend premium currency on low-impact refreshes without a plan.
Do not let AFK rewards cap when daily collection is available.
Do not overbuy generic materials while rare progression items remain.
modules.ctaLabel: Check the spend order, save list, and daily farming priorities
